text.en: Call 108 immediately and request a trauma-equipped ambulance.
text.hi: 108 पर तुरंत कॉल करें और ट्रॉमा एम्बुलेंस मांगें।

text.en: Apply firm direct pressure to any bleeding wound with a clean cloth.
text.hi: खून बहने वाले घाव पर साफ कपड़े से सीधा दबाव डालें।

text.en: Do NOT remove embedded objects; stabilise them in place.
text.hi: घाव में फँसी वस्तु को न निकालें; उसे यथास्थान रखें।

text.en: Keep the patient lying flat and warm.
text.hi: मरीज को सीधा लिटाएं और गर्म रखें।

text.en: Nothing by mouth — no food or water.
text.hi: कुछ भी खाने-पीने को न दें।

text.en: Monitor breathing and pulse every 2 minutes until handoff.
text.hi: हर 2 मिनट में साँस और नाड़ी जाँचते रहें।

asha_to_phc.en: TRAUMA ALERT — penetrating injury. Mechanism: {{mechanisms}}. Vitals: BP {{sbp}}/{{dbp}}, HR {{hr}}, SpO2 {{spo2}}. Direct pressure applied. 108 dispatched. Request blood and surgical readiness.

patient_instruction.hi: अभी अस्पताल जाना ज़रूरी है। 108 बुला ली है। शांत रहिए, हम साथ हैं।
patient_instruction.en: We need to go to the hospital right now. 108 has been called. Stay calm — I'm with you.

rules.rationale: Penetrating injuries — gunshot or stab wounds — carry an immediate risk of haemorrhagic shock and require trauma-centre care within the golden hour, well before any vital sign derangement is observed.

text.en: Apply firm direct pressure with a clean cloth for at least 10 minutes without lifting.
If a limb is bleeding, elevate it above the level of the heart while maintaining pressure.
Apply a tourniquet 5 cm above the wound only if bleeding cannot be controlled with pressure; note the exact time.
Call 108 immediately.
Lay patient flat, keep warm with a blanket, do not give food or water.

rules.rationale: Class III/IV haemorrhage (>=750–1500 mL) reliably produces shock; ASHA-level intervention is direct pressure and rapid transport. NHM Pre-hospital trauma module mandates 108 dispatch for any uncontrolled external bleeding.

text.en: Place patient in the recovery (lateral) position to protect airway; maintain manual jaw-thrust if breathing is noisy.
Keep neck immobilised; do not flex or rotate.
Call 108 — request neurology-capable facility.
Watch for vomiting; keep airway clear; recheck consciousness every 2 minutes.

rules.rationale: GCS <= 12 or any altered consciousness after head trauma indicates moderate-to-severe TBI and requires immediate neuroimaging-capable transfer.

text.en: Open airway: head-tilt, chin-lift (jaw-thrust if neck injury suspected).
Clear visible secretions/foreign body if reachable; do NOT do blind sweeps.
Place in recovery position once airway is open.
Call 108 immediately.

rules.rationale: Compromised airway and SpO2 < 90% are pre-arrest signs; any other priority (history, paperwork) must wait.

label.en: Major burn (>=15% body surface, face/airway, deep)

text.en: Cool the burn with running cool (not cold) water for at least 20 minutes.
Remove rings, bangles, watches before swelling; do NOT peel stuck cloth.
Cover the burn loosely with a clean dry cloth; do NOT apply oil/toothpaste/ash.
Encourage small sips of ORS if conscious and not vomiting.
Call 108 for any burn involving face, hands, genitals, or larger than the patient's palm.

rules.rationale: Domestic kitchen and stove burns are common in rural India; first 20 minutes of cool-water irrigation drastically reduces depth. NHM HBNC module Section 7.4 mandates non-occlusive clean dressing and prohibits traditional remedies.

text.en: Immobilise the limb in the position found using a splint, cardboard, or rolled cloth.
Elevate the limb if possible; apply a cool compress (no direct ice on skin).
Check pulse and sensation distal to the injury; if absent, escalate immediately.
Arrange transport to PHC/CHC for X-ray and orthopaedic review.

rules.rationale: Closed fractures rarely cause immediate mortality but require X-ray, reduction, and immobilisation; open fractures or absent distal pulses must be escalated to CRITICAL via airway/bleeding rules.

text.en: Lay the patient flat and elevate the legs 30 cm if no spinal/leg injury.
Cover with a blanket; do not let the patient become cold.
Call 108 immediately.
Nothing by mouth.

rules.rationale: Shock-index (HR/SBP) > 1 or SBP < 90 mmHg after trauma indicates compensated/early-decompensated shock; immediate transfer is mandatory regardless of mechanism.
